hook: AI gave me a 7-step framework when I just needed a gut check.
caption:
AI gave me a 7-step framework when I just needed a gut check.

I asked ChatGPT if I should take the client project.

It came back with a pros/cons matrix, three risk categories, a 'questions to consider' section, and a recommendation to 'weigh my personal values.'

I already knew the answer. I just wanted someone to confirm I wasn't crazy.

That's not an AI problem. That's a calibration problem.

Some people need the framework. They want the exhaustive breakdown before they can move.

Some people need one sharp sentence that cuts through the noise.

AI defaults to 'comprehensive' because comprehensive feels safe. But comprehensive isn't always useful — it's just long.

The people who get the most out of AI aren't the ones asking better questions.

They're the ones who've told AI what kind of thinker they are before the conversation even starts.

That's the whole idea behind BootFile. You build a thinking profile once. AI stops treating you like a generic user and starts responding like it actually knows you.

No more 7-step frameworks when you needed a gut check.

Link in bio if you want to build yours.
